keys.description: When true (default), foundation::ApplicationBooted dispatches SeedPlatformSportsCatalogJob synchronously when the sports table is empty. Off = platform must run `sports:registry:seed-platform-catalog` manually before any tenant lands. Off is only safe in migration harnesses where a bespoke seed handles it.

Bumps when `data/platform-sports-catalog.json` changes materially (new sports added, existing ones deactivated, taxonomy reshaped). Downstream consumers (tenant admin picker, ops dashboards) compare against the cached copy + refetch when the numbers diverge. Also drives the invalidation of the `sports:default_catalog:v{version}` cache key on deploy.

When true, SeedPlatformSportsCatalogJob is dispatched to a queue. When false (default), it runs synchronously at boot inside the foundation::ApplicationBooted handler. Sync is safer (no request lands before the catalog is present); async is available for boot-time performance tuning on large deployments. The DEFAULT is sync unlike most other modules — the catalog is a hard prerequisite for every Wave 3 write.
Cron expression for ReconcileSportReferencesJob. Nightly 04:15 UTC by default (staggered from other reconcilers: age-group 03:30, region 03:15, season 03:45).

Downstream tables the reconciler scans for dangling sport_key / discipline_key / position_key references. Wave 3a: only `teams` exists (via the concurrent Wave 2b teams module); Wave 3+ appends `athlete_enrollments`, `coaches`, `events`, `sessions`, `seasons`, `age_groups`, `facilities`. New Wave 3 modules append themselves here in their own config publish — no edit to this module needed.
Per-table column mapping — some tables carry the full triple (athlete_enrollments), others only sport_key. The reconciler iterates the tables in `reference_tables` + reads its columns from this map.

When true, drift (an unresolved sport_key / discipline_key / position_key) fires SportPrefixResolutionFailed + pages ops per `config('monitoring.channels.compliance')`. Off = drift is only logged; ops must poll audit_events.
Row chunk size when iterating a reference table. Keeps memory bounded when a tenant carries thousands of enrollments.
Platform-wide minimum for Sport.age_range_min. 3 is the practical floor (real programmes rarely include younger participants).
Platform-wide maximum for Sport.age_range_max. 99 is the sanity ceiling.
Slugs refused at write time. Reserved for internal tooling — any attempt to create a row with one of these slugs is rejected with SLUG_RESERVED (422).

When true (default), SportKeyResolver::resolve() returns deactivated rows when the active layer misses. Downstream historical enrollments referencing an old sport_key still resolve. Off = strict-active resolution; historical enrollments would fail with SPORT_KEY_UNRESOLVED (potentially breaking archived enrollment views).

When true, tenant customs shadow platform slugs during resolution (though the WRITE path forbids slug collisions, so this only matters in edge cases where the config changes mid-flight). Off = platform always wins.

Days a deactivated tenant custom row must remain quiet (is_active=false) before PurgeArchivedRegistryRowsJob is eligible to hard-delete it. Default 730 (2 years) — long enough that a re-activation is unlikely but not so long that the row becomes a permanent zombie. Only applies to tenant customs; platform-seeded rows are NEVER hard-deleted.

Days audit rows produced by this module survive after their source row is hard-deleted. Default 2555 (7 years) for SportPrefixResolutionFailed events (compliance replay). 730 days (2 years) for regular Registry mutations. Read-only reference — actual retention is enforced by the audit module's retention policy.
TTL for `sports:list:platform` — cached platform-seeded catalog. Long TTL because the platform catalog changes rarely (only on platform admin write or reseed).
TTL for `sports:list:tenant:{tenant_id}` — cached merged catalog (platform + tenant customs). Medium TTL because tenant customs may be added / edited more frequently.

TTL for `sports:resolve:{tenant_id}:{sport_key}:{discipline_key?}:{position_key?}` — cached resolver answers. Long TTL because slugs are immutable — the resolver answer for a given triple can only change when the underlying row is deactivated (which invalidates the key via observer).
